ARGUED AND THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND CONTINUES THE MATTER TO FEBRUARY 19, 2008, AT WHICH TIME THE COURT WILL TAKE THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL HAS FILED A DECLARATION RE CALCULATION OF PAYMENT PER FORMULA. COUNSEL ARE TO MEET AND CONFER TO SEE IF THEY CAN REACH AN AGREEMENT. ABSENT AN AGREEMENT, DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL IS TO FILE AN OPPOSITION BY FEBRUARY 19, 2008. BOTH SIDES ARE TO LODGE PROPOSED ORDERS BY FEBRUARY 19, 2008. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS PATRICIA JACKSON AND MARIAN JACKSON-CHAVEZ'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT. THIS MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. PREJUDGMENT INTEREST HAS NOT AND IS NOT BEING SOUGHT BY ROSE L. JACKSON. THE ORDER FOR APPORTIONMENT WAS THE JUDGMENT IN THIS CASE. SEE PASSAVANTI V. WILLIAMS (1990) 225 CAL. APP. 3D 1602, 1605-06. Appearances:
Eugene Schneider (510.547.2889) specially appearing for plaintiffs and responding parties
R.J. Bockelman and Robert Ferris (626.6975) for defendant and moving party

Mr. Ferris presented the Court with a proof of service as to plaintiff Marian Jackson Chavez. The matter was argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: denied. There was no basis to find contempt on the part of Marian Jackson Chavez. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-29-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS PATRICIA JACKSON AND MARIAN JACKSON-CHAVEZ'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT

Appearances:
Eugene Schneider (510.547.2889) for plaintiffs and moving parties
R.J. Bockelman and Robert Ferris (626.6975) for defendant and responding party

This matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Prejudgment interest has not and is not being sought by Rose L. Jackson.
